title: Find the Largest Palindrome Divisible by K
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal challenge: the single closed-form C++ construction is also the speed extreme and the memory extreme. Per the repo standard (no coincident files), only solution.cpp is shipped; there is no separate solution-runtime or solution-memory file.

variants.languageReason: C++ gives native-speed construction of one string with length up to 100000 and direct in-place edits for the few digits forced by divisibility. It avoids interpreter, VM, and GC overhead while using only O(1) auxiliary state beyond the returned string.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Selected for direct std::string allocation, native loops, and a clean LeetCode class signature.
Considered for manual memory control, but it still must allocate the same output buffer and adds platform-boundary risk without improving the asymptotic footprint.
Considered for native performance and safety, but string conversion ergonomics add implementation overhead with no performance gain for this fixed-rule construction.
Considered for compiled byte-slice construction, but runtime and GC overhead are less attractive than C++ for an output-bound top-runtime target.
Considered because managed runtimes can build O(n) strings, but string-builder allocation and JIT/GC behavior add avoidable overhead.
Considered for concise string repetition, but VM/interpreter overhead is unnecessary at n up to 100000 when C++ can emit the result directly.

variants.approachJustification: Use divisor-specific closed forms for k <= 9. Keep all dominant digits as 9, lower only the forced edge or central digits, and handle k = 7 with a central block table derived from 10^6 == 1 (mod 7).

reasoningSummary: For fixed length, the largest palindrome is obtained by keeping the earliest digits as high as possible. Small k allows direct divisibility rules: endpoints for 2/4/5/8, digit-sum correction for 6, all 9s for 1/3/9, and a tiny central-block table for 7 based on the period of powers of ten modulo 7.
